decision: Keep the API server on Hono
rationale: Hono's middleware model covers auth, logging and rate limiting with minimal overhead, and it runs identically on Node and edge runtimes

rejected.reason: Larger dependency surface and slower cold starts; the middleware ecosystem is tempting but everything we need exists for Hono. Migration was evaluated and declined 2026-03
No measured gain over Hono for our workload; migration cost with zero user-visible benefit

decision: Use Zustand for client state in the dashboard
rationale: Our global state surface is small (session, filters, toasts); Zustand covers it in ~50 lines without ceremony

rejected.reason: Boilerplate and indirection outweigh benefits at our state size; the team spent more time on slices than features in the spike
Implicit reactivity made renders hard to trace during the spike

decision: Query Postgres with the postgres.js driver and hand-written SQL
rationale: Direct SQL keeps query plans visible and avoids a runtime abstraction layer; typed row mappers live next to each query

rejected.reason: Query engine adds cold-start latency on serverless deploys and hides generated SQL; we hit both in the 2026-02 spike
Decorator-based entities fought our functional codebase style

decision: Use native fetch with a small retry wrapper for outbound HTTP
rationale: Node 18+ ships fetch; one 40-line wrapper gives retries, timeouts and JSON handling without a dependency

rejected.reason: Interceptor chains obscured a double-retry bug in 2026-01; native fetch plus our wrapper is smaller and easier to audit
Deprecated upstream since 2020; unpatched vulnerabilities

decision: Store session tokens in httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookies
rationale: Keeps tokens out of reach of injected scripts; the browser attaches them automatically so the SPA never touches the raw token

rejected.reason: XSS exfiltration — the 2026-04 pentest demonstrated token theft through a compromised analytics snippet reading localStorage
Same XSS exposure as localStorage, plus tokens lost on every new tab

decision: Run unit tests with the built-in node:test runner
rationale: Zero test-framework dependencies; node --test runs TypeScript output directly and CI needs no transform config

rejected.reason: ESM support still needs transform workarounds; config drift across packages burned two days in the 2026-03 attempt
Capable, but node:test covers our needs without another dependency to track

decision: Style the dashboard with Tailwind utility classes
rationale: No runtime style computation, works with React Server Components, and design tokens live in one config

rejected.reason: Runtime CSS-in-JS is incompatible with React Server Components and added ~12KB to the bundle in the spike
Workable but splits styling across files; team preferred colocated utilities

decision: Cache session lookups in a Postgres UNLOGGED table
rationale: Keeps the stack at exactly one datastore; UNLOGGED tables benchmarked within 15% of Redis for our session read pattern

rejected.reason: A second datastore to provision, monitor, back up and secure — ops budget decision is one datastore until scale forces otherwise (revisit at 10x traffic)
Breaks under multiple API instances; sticky sessions rejected as deploy constraint

decision: Hand-written, numbered, forward-only SQL migrations
rationale: Every schema change is reviewed as literal SQL; forward-only keeps prod and CI history linear

rejected.reason: A generated diff silently included a column drop that reached staging in 2026-03 — rolled back; generated DDL is no longer trusted for schema changes
False confidence — most destructive changes cannot actually be reversed; restore-from-backup is the real rollback

decision: Public API stays REST with an OpenAPI spec
rationale: HTTP caching, straightforward auth, and every client language has mature tooling; sparse fieldsets cover the overfetching complaints

rejected.reason: Resolver N+1s and cache invalidation complexity outweigh flexibility for our ~30-endpoint surface; evaluated 2026-05 with the mobile team present
Public consumers are not all TypeScript; locks the contract to one language ecosystem

decision: Structured logging with pino, one JSON line per request
rationale: Fast, structured, and the ops dashboards parse JSON lines natively

decision: CI runs on GitHub Actions with a single reusable workflow
rationale: One workflow file, matrix per package; no CI vendor lock beyond what the repo host already implies

decision: pnpm workspace for the monorepo
rationale: Strict node_modules layout catches phantom dependencies; workspace protocol keeps internal packages linked

decision: Token-bucket rate limiting at the API edge, per API key
rationale: Predictable burst behavior; limits configured per plan tier

decision: All API errors use the problem+json envelope
rationale: RFC 7807 gives clients one error shape; internal codes map to documented types

decision: Local development runs through docker compose
rationale: Postgres and the API start with one command; parity with the deploy image

decision: Prettier with the shared config; ESLint flat config for lint rules
rationale: Formatting is not reviewed; lint rules are; keeping the tools separate keeps both fast

decision: Feature flags read from environment variables, no flag service
rationale: A dozen flags do not justify a flag platform; env vars deploy atomically with the code

decision: OpenTelemetry traces exported to the collector, sampled at 10%
rationale: Vendor-neutral instrumentation; sampling keeps ingest costs flat

decision: User file uploads go to S3-compatible object storage, never the database
rationale: Keeps the database small and backups fast; presigned URLs offload transfer

decision: Zod schemas validate every API input at the boundary
rationale: One schema per endpoint doubles as the OpenAPI source of truth

decision: API is versioned in the URL path (/v1), breaking changes bump the version
rationale: Explicit and cache-friendly; header versioning confused two integration partners in the spike

decision: Each package keeps a README with run and test instructions
rationale: Onboarding doc lives next to the code it describes

decision: UI strings go through the i18n catalog from day one
rationale: Retrofitting i18n was a six-week project at the last company; catalog-first is cheap now
